canonical_topology.role: pure platform logic and state transitions
thin operator-facing CLI and app entrypoints
repo certification engines and rule families
generated registries, bindings, and help surfaces
canonical Rust orchestration runtime program root
canonical Rust repo operations control-plane program root

typescript_allowed_for: bounded glue that does not become the canonical engine

source_of_truth_policy: tools/contracts/** is the only machine-readable source of truth for Repo Operations Platform contracts
tools/generated/** is the only source of truth for generated command alias registries and projections
The zero-gap end state permits no duplicated TypeScript and Rust semantic-validator ownership
repoctl CLI plus repoctld own coordination truth; stable Bun aliases and repo-local governance skills remain client surfaces only
.git/repoctl/state.sqlite is runtime state only and must never become a handwritten contract surface
zero_gap_completion_criteria: All top-level /tools roots are declared canonical platform segments or canonical platform program roots
Repo-wide scanner and runtime engines converge on Rust as the canonical implementation where the engine_policy requires it
TypeScript entrypoints are thin adapters or generated writers only and do not own duplicated policy engines
Machine-readable Repo Operations Platform contracts live only under tools/contracts/**
Command alias projections remain generated from tools/generated/** and are not hand-owned in parallel
repoctl CLI plus repoctld are the only canonical coordination owners for task, session, bundle, worktree, doctor, and cleanup semantics
Repo-local governance skills describe repoctl CLI usage and do not mutate shared repo state directly
Stable Bun aliases remain thin adapters over repoctl and do not own coordination truth
repoctld owns the only local task/worktree/source/cleanup coordination path
.git/repoctl/state.sqlite is the only durable local coordination source of truth
Task liveness and write exclusivity derive from explicit session leases instead of worktree residue

coordination_owner.current_state: repoctld owns session leases, daemon-backed task issue source/bundle resolution, control-plane reconcile/doctor/cleanup, and worktree control-plane commands; repoctl CLI is the canonical operator and agent surface, while stable Bun wrappers and repo-local governance skills only compose pure validators and generated read-model materialization over daemon-owned coordination primitives.
coordination_owner.ideal_state: repoctld is the single local coordination owner behind stable task/worktree operator aliases and agent clients.

durable_state.current_state: coordination durability lives in .git/repoctl/state.sqlite for task bundles, session leases, runtime records, doctor reports, and cleanup history; branch-scoped snapshots/manifests and repo-wide catalogs are generated read models rather than writable coordination truth.
durable_state.ideal_state: .git/repoctl/state.sqlite stores the canonical durable task bundle, session lease, runtime record, doctor report, and cleanup history surfaces.

lease_model.current_state: explicit session leases determine write exclusivity, while worktree residue and recent activity only classify cleanup safety and rescue state.
lease_model.ideal_state: explicit session leases with heartbeat, expiry, release, and quarantine semantics determine liveness and exclusivity.

mixed_engine_surfaces.current_state: task/worktree clients are thin wrappers plus pure validators/read-model emitters over repoctl-owned coordination flows; no parallel client-owned coordination engine remains.
mixed_engine_surfaces.ideal_state: task/worktree governance runtime converges on repoctld with thin client adapters only

gap_inventory.current_state: tools/orchestrator and tools/repoctl are canonical Rust program roots declared by the Repo Operations Platform topology instead of legacy exceptions.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: Every top-level /tools root is either a canonical platform segment or a canonical platform program root.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: No undeclared legacy top-level /tools roots remain.

gap_inventory.current_state: repoctld now owns session lease arbitration, coordination digests, and daemon-backed control-plane RPC for task/worktree/source/cleanup flows.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: repoctld owns task session lease arbitration, coordination digests, and local control-plane RPC behind stable aliases and agent clients.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: The local daemon is the only live coordination owner for task/worktree/source/cleanup arbitration.

gap_inventory.current_state: repoctld persists task bundles, runtime records, doctor reports, and cleanup history in SQLite, and the remaining branch-scoped snapshots/manifests plus repo-wide catalogs are generated read models rather than durable coordination SoT.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: .git/repoctl/state.sqlite is the only durable local source of truth for daemon-owned coordination state.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: Local coordination durability lives only in SQLite rather than in parallel file/residue surfaces.

gap_inventory.current_state: Session leases determine liveness/exclusivity, and worktree residue or recent file activity only feed safe-cleanup versus requires-intervention classification.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: Active lease, released lease, expired lease, quarantined residue, safe cleanup, and requires-intervention are distinct machine-readable states.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: Worktree residue no longer acts as the canonical liveness or exclusivity source.

gap_inventory.current_state: task/worktree/source/cleanup clients, stable Bun aliases, and repo-local governance skills now act as thin adapters or pure validator/read-model emitters over repoctl-owned coordination primitives instead of parallel control-plane owners.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: Those surfaces are thin clients over repoctld and do not own policy or state transitions themselves.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: No task/worktree/source/cleanup client, stable Bun alias, or repo-local governance skill remains a parallel canonical owner.

gap_inventory.current_state: Repo-wide scanner family commands now route through repoctl or Rust canonical engines, and the remaining TypeScript check commands are explicitly bounded adapters or generated-artifact glue.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: Repo-wide scanner families use Rust as the canonical engine and keep TypeScript limited to thin adapters or bounded glue.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: Repo-wide scanner families no longer rely on TypeScript as the canonical policy engine.

gap_inventory.current_state: Rust owns the canonical session/state/control-plane contracts, and TypeScript surfaces consume delegated runtime outputs or thin wrappers instead of re-owning the same machine validators.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: One canonical semantic-validator owner exists per machine contract, with the other language consuming generated or delegated artifacts instead of duplicating logic.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: No machine contract keeps duplicated TypeScript and Rust semantic-validator ownership.

gap_inventory.current_state: Operator-facing command aliases now route through tools/apps thin entrypoints instead of pointing directly at tools/orchestrator or tools/repoctl residency.
gap_inventory.ideal_state: Operator entrypoints live in tools/apps and other ownership lives in the canonical segment that matches its role.
gap_inventory.zero_gap_condition: Operator entrypoints and runtime helpers no longer require legacy-root residency.
